authority: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-architecture-plan.md — the plan IS the authority. This manifest is a derived index of plan §11 (Execution Order — Two Waves) for machine consumption by the akm-090-chunk workflow. If §11 changes, regenerate this file; on any conflict the plan wins. Section references of the form §15.N mean numbered RULE N of plan §15 (the plan has no §15 sub-headings).
planVersion: final scope pass, commit 5555616 (Tier-A only, lifecycle deferred, no trust machinery, reported ledger, hygiene-first waves)
grepGateScope: src/ + scripts/ + src/assets/, EXCLUDING src/migrate/legacy/ (the frozen §3.4 copy retains dead identifiers by design — plan §11 gate note) — tests are driven to zero by the §15.2 ratchet on the same identifiers; docs by the Chunk 10 sweep
globalGates: Zero-count greps at declared scope (plan §11), each IN EFFECT ONLY FROM the chunk that deletes its referent onward — a gate runner applies a grep only if its effective chunk is this chunk or earlier in manifest order (order: 0a, 7, 6, 9, 0b, 1, 1.5, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6.5, 8, 10); before that it is out of scope, not red: AkmAssetType (from Chunk 1.5); TYPE_DIRS, resolveAssetPathFromName, runMatchers, getAssetTypes, ASSET_SPECS, LINTER_MAP (from Chunk 3); wikiName + the wiki type token (from Chunk 4); StashEntry, parseAssetRef, .stash.json/loadStashFile (from Chunk 5); resolveStashDir outside the RunContext builder (from Chunk 9)

Safety suites port-first and green at EVERY chunk boundary (plan §15.3): traversal/escape, symlink, redaction/dangerous-key, SQLite journal/busy/lock/contention/cross-proc, migration suites. Fixed points not to touch: tests/_helpers/sandbox.ts, tests/_preload.ts, the mock.module-ban lint, hand-rolled sharding
Every chunk lands its named §15 test bucket in the same chunk; chunk gate is not green until its bucket is (plan §15.1)

Every chunk commits its per-chunk deletion ledger — a HARD gate (plan §1.3: 'the hard gates are the zero-count greps, the per-chunk deletion ledgers, and the parity/contract/behavior tests'; §11 preamble; DoD 8)
The net-LOC NUMBER (signed adds + deletions) is REPORTED per chunk, never a pass/fail threshold (plan §1.3, DoD 8)
No new trust/approval/security machinery; memory lifecycle deferred entirely; anything lifecycle-shaped or trust-shaped in a diff is a scope-creep BLOCKER (plan §1.3, §12.4)
Local green gate per chunk: typecheck + unit + affected integration + safety suites (plan §11); bun run check is the full-suite superset used when no affected-only selection exists

chunks.title: Hygiene goldens
chunks.scope: Behavior baselines for the surfaces Wave 1 touches: improve-run output goldens (the §5 behavior-change ledger baseline — self-consistency call counts, P0-A selection sets), proposal/transaction round-trip goldens (all three journal engines), CLI output baselines for the commands the sweep rewires. Goldens grammar-agnostic where possible (counts/outcomes, not raw refs — §12.4 Wave-1 rebase friction).
gates: Golden fixtures committed
All plan line anchors this chunk relies on re-measured at HEAD before capture (§12.4 line-drift risk)
chunks.testBucket: §15.5 goldens (capture-only; each asset designated frozen vs re-baseline); §15.7 consolidate behavior-preservation goldens captured here

chunks.title: improve decomposition + dead-lane deletions
chunks.scope: Decompose the 4 improve god fns + the consolidate ops (planConsolidation/handleMergeOp/handlePromoteOp → named passes; existing behavior preserved EXACTLY per §6 — no lifecycle state model, deferred). Adopt three-verb naming (revise/learn/consolidate — vocabulary only). Delete self-consistency/exploration/calibration/procedural/autotune/review_pressure/ValenceScore.lane/P0-A/multi-cycle with the §5 behavior-change ledger attached. Trim promotion-policy literal. RunContext into processSession + appendEvent ctx adoption (§10.7). Finish-or-delete structured-call (§10.7). Serializer/resolveParentRef consolidation. PLUS §13.1 fold-ins assigned to this chunk: delete the recombine/synthesis cross-episodic subsystem (recombine.ts ~1,009 LOC, recombine-repository.ts ~290, the migrations.ts:679 table — supersedes §5's earlier keep-as-learn-recipe wording; add to this chunk's deletion ledger and grep-gate) and, per §14.3 in the SAME chunk, the recombine-orphaned index-DB exports getEntitiesByEntryIds (db.ts:1109) and getNeighborsByEntryId (db.ts:896) — not left as dead index-DB APIs. The ~−1,300 for the fold-ins is ledgered under §12.1's residual row, outside this chunk's ~−3900.
gates: §5 behavior-change ledger attached to the chunk

Consolidate behavior preservation vs Chunk 0a goldens — merge/delete/promote/contradict outcomes, journal round-trip, hot-capture guard, contradiction preserve-and-qualify, byte-for-byte where deterministic (§12.3, DoD 5)
No lifecycle state model — states/water-marks/pressure/CAS/purge are deferred and must not slip in (§6, §12.4)
No god fn >~200 LOC remains in improve (DoD 5)
chunks.testBucket: §15.7 consolidate behavior-preservation (replay Chunk 0a goldens); §15.1 per-chunk pairing for deleted lanes

chunks.title: Proposal → FileChange[] + one transaction
chunks.scope: Collapse 3 FS journal engines into one FileChange transaction (preserve fsync + before-hash). Proposal{changes: FileChange[]}. Dedup/cooldown → fingerprints (with model-id term) + retained rejection backoff in this same chunk (§4.5). Delete the confidence gate. bulkAdjudicateProposals. Runs on the CURRENT ref grammar; proposal keys re-key at cutover like all state.
gates: Journal dirs removed
One-transaction fault tests green — mid-apply fault leaves no partial write, before-hash abort (§12.3)
chunks.testBucket: §15.7 transaction fault injection; §15.5 proposal/transaction round-trip goldens from Chunk 0a stay green
chunks.notes: Moved to Wave 2 (Chunk 5) — do NOT do here: export #fragment refs, the parseAssetRef→0 gate, and the legacy-import→migrator fold (the migrator home doesn't exist until Chunk 1).

chunks.title: Cross-cutting sweep
chunks.scope: Ambient RunContext threading (retire the 18 _set*ForTests seams). Config discriminated schemas + reserved-knob deletion. Output helpers/shape-registry dedup. Health/tasks god decomposition. cli argv normalization to one ParsedInvocation (§10.7) + duration residue + caps(×10)/homeDir/mirror/session-log/spawn/semver/connection dedup. npm.path() + parseSourceSpec fixes. Typed-error sweep (§10.7). Import-cycle workstream (§10.7): Wave 1 kills the non-taxonomy cycles (improve-types, config↔integrations, harness/tasks barrels); CI gate lands as a RATCHET (no new cycles, count monotonically decreasing; hits 0 when Chunk 3 deletes the taxonomy cycles). Delete stale database.ts:14 comment + add Node 24 to node-smoke/release-gates matrices. PLUS §13.1 fold-ins assigned to this chunk: vendored echarts.min.js → CDN default-flip and drop the vendored asset (−1 MB; a default flip of html-report.ts:41/:383, not an implementation — §14.1); deterministic-embedder relocation of TEST SCAFFOLDING ONLY (−60) — the AKM_EMBED_DETERMINISTIC env-gated switch STAYS in the production embedder facade (embedder.ts:120,154): scripts/akm-eval and the §13.2 measurement pass depend on it; core/eval/rank-metrics.ts → scripts/akm-eval (−180); the --format html generic template framework → health-only render (−160). PLUS §14.2 fold-ins: H1 AkmHarness capability-discriminated union (retire the load-time throw), H2 health-report view-model/HTML split (deeper than §4.7's line-count split), H3 scheduleKillLadder covering the in-file spawn.ts:527,545 copies, L1 text/registry.ts fold. PLUS §14.3: delete AkmHarness.resume + the *_RESUME_FLAG constants. Fold-ins are ledgered under §12.1's residual row, outside this chunk's ~−2000.
gates: grep resolveStashDir → residual only in the RunContext builder
Import-cycle ratchet armed in CI (no new cycles; count monotonically decreasing)
chunks.testBucket: §15.1 per-chunk pairing (seam-retirement test updates land with each seam); CLI output baselines from Chunk 0a stay green

The frozen legacy copy INTENTIONALLY retains dead identifiers (per-type ASSET_SPECS, TYPE_DIRS walks — §3.4); src/migrate/legacy/ is therefore excluded from the zero-count grep scope (plan §11 gate note, amended 2026-07-14) — do NOT rename identifiers inside the frozen copy to appease greps.

chunks.title: IndexDocument + ref grammar + db.ts split
chunks.scope: Rename StashEntry → IndexDocument + provenance + pinned signal columns (adapter spec §3). The [bundle//]conceptId grammar lands HERE: anchored body-ref form, REF_RE retarget, export #fragment refs, parseAssetRef deletion. Schema-column migration (entry_key/stash_dir/entry_type/entry_json → new column set) with utility/usage re-keyed onto item_ref. Diff persistence (upsert-by-ref, drain-before-transaction, zero-document preflight — adapter spec §4) replaces truncate paths (#624-P1 lesson). Split db.ts into storage repos + jsonColumn() helper; invert storage↔indexer arrow. Unify scored/enumerate filter path. L0/L1/L2 derived index artifacts (cards/outlines per normative §15.2). item_links table + consumers. Fold .stash.json legacyOverrides + mergeLegacyEntry + pre-0.9 proposal legacy-import → migrator.

chunks.title: Activation policy (Tier A — install≠activate consolidation)
chunks.scope: Consolidate today's scattered install≠activation enforcement — the registryId first-party/third-party block-vs-warn (env-binding.ts:110-121), the add-time dangerous-key scan (add-cli.ts:74-215), task enabled: state (YAML + runner.ts:159 fire-time check), and writable (search-source.ts:35) — into ONE workspace activation-policy point. Confirm-by-test that installing a bundle with tasks/env/workflows grants nothing until an explicit enable. These are PORTS of existing behavior. env/secret handling UNCHANGED. Accepted-by-design residual (documented, not gated): workflow refs resolve across installed sources and re-read current disk content per invocation — crontab semantics. DEFERRED to Tier B indefinitely: workspace_bindings record, digests, rebind-on-update, akm bind|unbind|bindings CLI.
gates: Install≠activate port-preservation tests green (§12.3, DoD 10): install grants nothing; explicit enable grants exactly what it grants today; env injection from a registry-installed source still hard-blocks dangerous keys AFTER the config migration
No new trust/approval machinery: no labeling, no action clamps, no confirm prompts, no digests, no trust records (§1.3)
Scope tripwire: if threading source/trust context to the task runner or native executor exceeds ~150 LOC, STOP and re-scope — do not creep toward the record (§12.4)
chunks.netLoc: +200 to +400 (budgeted add — decision logic consolidates; interactive confirm/rollback UX in add-cli stays where it is)

chunks.scope: The §3.3 journaled cutover-as-code: workflow DDL fold + usage_events rescue (it lives in index.db!) + full §3.2 re-key + orphan quarantine (expected orphans → legacy_state quarantine; integrity failures → fail-closed) + ATTACH sequence (ATTACH outside txn; verified empirically on bun:sqlite 3.51). NO bindings/lifecycle DDL (§3.2). Delete workflows/db.ts + workflowDb locations/paths (frozen WORKFLOW_MIGRATIONS copy retained, §8.2). index.db quarantine-rename + out-of-gate rebuild. Config migration stashDir/sources[]/installed[]/wikiName → bundles/defaultBundle + bundle lock state (normative §10.2) — SUPERSEDES the per-source integrations/lockfile.ts shape; the bindings: config map is Tier B — NOT emitted (mirrored in Chunk 10's schemas item). report.ts barrier-fn decomposition with the W2 finalize-lock split (§10.7). Throwaway migrator @removeIn next-minor.
gates: 4 → 3 DBs
